data.title: Teleperformance Stock: Undercovered, Underappreciated, Why It Remains A Strong Buy
data.description:
Introduction

I wrote my first article on multinational business services provider Teleperformance SE (OTCPK:TLPFF, OTCPK:TLPFY) in November 2023, and although I liked the fundamentals and felt it was a contrarian play, the valuation did not convince me. But about five months later — the stock had fallen 80% from what was certainly an exuberant all-time high — I felt the time was right to build a position in TP shares (see this article).

The year 2024 has been more or less volatile for Teleperformance (recently rebranded TP), despite fundamentally solid operational progress. The company reported its full-year 2024 results yesterday, and judging by the immediate reaction, the results did not impress the market. As I will explain in this update, I see things differently.

In addition to a look at growth — both from a group and segment perspective — I will also discuss the profitability of Teleperformance following the consolidation of Majorel more than a year ago. I will also give an update on financial stability, against the backdrop of what, I believe, could indeed be a “higher for longer” environment. Finally, I will update my discounted cash flow valuation model and reveal whether I will increase my position in Teleperformance shares going forward.

However, I will not go into detail on the governance aspects related to the succession of founder and CEO Daniel Julien and the appointment of Moulay Elalamy as Chairman of the Board of Directors, mainly because I have dedicated an entire article to this topic.

Teleperformance 2024 Earnings Review – A Lot To Like, And A Small Hair In The Soup

To begin with, it should be noted that 2024 was the first year in which Majorel was fully consolidated and that the company presented a comparison based on the adjusted figures for 2023.

While actual revenue growth was 23%, organic growth was 2.6%. It is worth noting that growth accelerated over the course of the year. While growth in the first half of the year was only 1.7%, the company reported growth of 3.0% and 4.0% for the third and fourth quarters respectively. This is better than the overall growth of 2.4% expected for the second half of the year when the half-year results were published. Diluted adjusted earnings per share (EPS) amounted to €13.44 and were therefore 8.5% higher than in the previous year. Diluted EPS fell by 35%, mainly due to amortization of acquired intangible assets (approx. €3.7 per share), restructuring costs (approx. €1.9 per share) and impairment of goodwill (approx. €0.5 per share).

Core Services (see my first article for a detailed description of the business model) contributed 71% to adjusted EBITA (earnings before interest, taxes, and amortization) in 2024, while Specialized Services contributed 29%. The contribution of Specialized Services increased by two percentage points compared to the previous year, which is very encouraging given the more than twice as strong profitability (30.0% margin vs. 12.4% margin). Revenue growth in Specialized Services and Core Services amounted to 10.1% and 1.4% respectively on a pro forma/organic basis.

From a regional perspective and on a constant currency basis, the EMEA segment outperformed the Americas segment by far. Revenue in the Americas Core Services segment fell by 0.8% year-over-year, while the EMEA segment grew by 3.5%. The current weakness of the Americas segment should be kept in mind going forward — also because this segment accounts for more than 40% of Teleperformance's consolidated revenue. Nevertheless, I was pleased to read in the report that LanguageLine Solution in the U.S. (part of Specialized Services) showed a strong improvement in operating margin compared to 2023.

According to the management, the integration of Majorel is proceeding as expected, which is reflected, for example, in the virtually stable EBITA margin of 15% (a slight increase of 10 basis points year-over-year). However, investors should note that EBITA for 2024 has been adjusted for costs of €112 million for synergy generation related to the integration of Majorel and the reorganization of the business in France. Without this adjustment, the EBITA margin would have been one percentage point lower. Nevertheless, I consider this adjustment to be appropriate, but it is of course important to pay attention to potentially recurring adjustments in this direction going forward. Other items excluded from EBITA were goodwill impairments (which are not really significant in 2023 or 2024) and stock-based compensation.

As the latter is quite significant (8.4% of 2024 operating profit), I will include it — as in my previous analysis — in my assessment of the company's free cash flow and consequently in my estimate of the leverage ratio and also in the valuation.

Mainly thanks to the acquisition of Majorel, but also due to the continued solid underlying performance, TP generated gross operating cash flow (OCF) of €1.8 billion, 32% more than in 2023. It should be noted, however, that due to the relevant reporting standards, OCF is based on operating profit and not net profit. Considering working capital movements, excluding interest and lease payments, and including the above-mentioned stock-based compensation as a cash expense, free cash flow (FCF) amounted to around €850 million, 32% more than in the previous year.

Profitability — based on the adjusted operating result and free cash flow — fell slightly compared to the previous year (Figure 1). However, at 11% and 8%, profitability remains in line with the long-term average. The market hypothesis that Teleperformance's business model is being eroded by AI offerings from tech giants such as Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) or Alphabet Inc. (GOOG, GOOGL) cannot be confirmed by this observation, and also against the backdrop of the organic revenue growth.

It was really reassuring to see that TP's ability to generate meaningful returns on capital remains intact despite the large acquisition. Both return on invested capital (ROIC) and cash ROIC were around 9% in 2024 (Figure 2), slightly above the estimated weighted average cost of capital and cost of equity, respectively.

Teleperformance has prudently utilized what, I believe, is a strong free cash flow in 2024. Most importantly, net debt has decreased by more than €600 million, resulting in a leverage ratio of 5.0x or 3.7x free cash flow, depending on whether lease liabilities are considered or not (Figure 3). The leverage ratio, measured by net debt in relation to EBITDA before impairments, was very reasonable at 2.4x. Based on management's figures, net debt to recurring EBITDA ratio at the end of 2024 was 1.9x. The lower net debt (and also gross debt) should lead to a significant improvement in Teleperformance's interest coverage ratio, a metric I was somewhat skeptical about in my first article (Figure 7 therein).

In addition to the debt paydown, Teleperformance also repurchased shares worth €184 million, representing almost 3% of the diluted shares outstanding following the acquisition of Majorel. Management did not disclose the 2024 diluted weighted average shares outstanding (DWASO) in the press release, so it is not directly possible to assess the impact of stock-based compensation and therefore the “effectiveness” of the buyback. However, putting the group's reported net profit in relation to reported diluted EPS, DWASO in 2024 can be estimated at 60.0 million. Considering that based on DWASO for 2023, which was determined in the same way (and confirmed via the 2023 annual report, p. 320), there were 59.1 million diluted shares outstanding, it is clear that there is even a slight dilution. This is not surprising given the company's reliance on stock-based compensation, but certainly somewhat disappointing.

As a conservative investor, I welcome the current emphasis on debt repayment, but would like to see more impactful share buybacks if the debt metrics allow and of course assuming the discounted valuation remains in place (as discussed in the Conclusion).

Finally, Teleperformance also announced the acquisition of ZP Better Together, LLC in November 2024. The acquisition of this leading provider of speech solutions for the deaf and hard of hearing was completed just a few weeks ago and should further boost growth in the Specialized Services segment. As ZP is a U.S.-focused company, I will be looking closely at the reported growth of Specialized Services going forward for signs of underperformance that could be masked by ZP's contribution. ZP is expected to have generated $230 million in revenue in 2024, which is about 13% of TP's Specialized Services segment after its hypothetical consolidation.

However, I would like to emphasize that I am not implying any bad intentions by management at this point. In my opinion, the consolidation of Majorel and the impact on growth was communicated transparently.

With this in mind, I was somewhat suspicious of the fact that the loss of a major contract in visa application management (part of Specialized Services) was excluded from the 2025 like-for-like revenue growth guidance. Given the typically high churn in Teleperformance's business (to be fair, however, this is particularly true for Core Services), I would have preferred such an adjustment to have been avoided. However, considering that management highlighted this adjustment in a footnote on page 1 of the earnings press release and directly on page 2 when discussing the 2025 outlook, I am actually satisfied with the quality of the reporting in terms of transparency.

Considering this lost contract as “business as usual”, Teleperformance expects comparable revenue growth — i.e., excluding the impact of ZP — of 2 to 4%. This may not sound like much, but I think it is reasonable, especially against the economic backdrop and particularly considering competition. Teleperformance expects a very modest improvement in profitability in 2025 and will continue to reduce debt. For 2024, the Board of Directors has proposed a dividend of €4.20 per share (up 9.2% year-over-year, Figure 4). Teleperformance shares thus offer a starting dividend yield of around 4.5%. However, bear in mind the French withholding tax on dividends.

Conclusion: Why Teleperformance Stock Could Remain In The Doldrums But Is A Strong Buy Nonetheless

The France-based leader in the field of business process outsourcing (BPO) Teleperformance SE published its annual results yesterday, February 27. I was pleased to see that revenue growth exceeded management's guidance, despite an already improved outlook for the second half of the year communicated in July. For 2024, the company achieved adjusted earnings per share of €13.44, or €807 at group level, based on revenue of €10.3 billion, which increased by 2.6% on an organic basis. The adjustments to earnings per share were understandable, although I will closely monitor the discrepancy between IFRS and reported earnings for potentially inappropriate adjustments in the future.

The integration of Majorel is progressing as expected, which is also reflected in the continued solid free cash flow profitability. Working capital provided a significant boost to reported free cash flow in 2024, so a close look at the individual accounts is a prudent move when the 2024 annual report becomes available. However, even if acquired receivables made a significant contribution in 2024, it is reassuring that free cash flow remained very healthy even after smoothing out working capital movements.

Thanks to the solid operating performance — which is also reflected in a continued significant ROIC and CROIC — Teleperformance was able to reduce net debt by more than €600 million year-over-year. The share buybacks in 2024 only managed to offset part of the (small) dilution, which was a bit of a downer, but as a conservative investor, the focus on deleveraging is welcome despite what, I believe, is a very compelling valuation. Going forward, however, I would like to see an increased focus on share buybacks beyond just offsetting dilution.

In terms of valuation, the market remains very skeptical about Teleperformance's future. TLPFY — the ADRs for Teleperformance — are currently trading at 7 times adjusted earnings per ADR (Figure 5). If analysts' estimates are correct for the coming years, investors could be looking at an annualized return of more than 20% without any contribution from multiple expansion. A return to a still modest earnings multiple of 10 could result in an annualized return of more than 30%.

However, I personally think it is unlikely that TP shares will return to their long-term average P/E ratio of 20 given what I believe could be a permanent impairment of sentiment (see my previous articles). However, this means that share buybacks represent a very compelling return on investment going forward and therefore act as a significant tailwind for EPS growth. In some ways, Teleperformance reminds me of tobacco company Imperial Brands plc (OTCQX:IMBBY, IMBBF - my articles here), which was shunned for many years until the market finally started to wake up.

Finally, let me briefly discuss my updated discounted cash flow valuation model, which takes into account the current diluted shares outstanding and adjusted free cash flow for 2024. Based on near-term expected revenue growth and assuming free cash flow profitability proves stable, TP shares would be worth about €140 today at a cost of equity of 11% and a terminal growth rate of only 1%. In other words, the current price of TP stock implies a cost of equity of more than 16% at the growth rates shown in Figure 6.

However, given the uncertainty about the long-term future of TP, I think it is advisable to also model a strong bearish scenario to quantify the margin of safety. This is an approach that I take a lot of comfort in when investing in potentially challenged sectors. In this context, I would like to draw your attention to a separate article in which I explain this approach in the context of investments in tobacco companies.

For the case at hand, I have modeled a slowdown in growth of one percentage point per year until a floor of 5% decline in free cash flow per year is reached and maintained. As shown in Figure 7, TP shares have priced in an even more pessimistic outlook at the selected cost of equity of 11%. In other words, Teleperformance could actually cease operations in 2035 and would still generate an annualized return of 7% based on this model. As with all value stocks that generate significant current free cash flow, it is reassuring to see that even in this very pessimistic scenario, less than a quarter of the company's value is based on free cash flows beyond 2034.

Taken together, I maintain my “Strong Buy” rating for TP stock and expect to add more shares to the position I started a year ago. However, due to the uncertainties, Teleperformance remains a comparatively small position in my portfolio, which I limit at no more than 0.75% of my portfolio value in terms of invested capital.

Thank you very much for reading my latest article. Whether you agree or disagree with my conclusions, I always welcome your opinion and feedback in the comments below. And if there's anything I should improve or expand on in future articles, drop me a line as well. As always, please consider this article only as a first step in your own due diligence.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: American Tower's Turnaround Is Here - Fade The Rally First
data.description:
AMT Remains Attractively Valued – Offering Opportunistic Investors With The Dual Pronged Returns

We previously covered American Tower Corporation (NYSE:AMT) in January 2025, discussing why its sell-off had been well warranted, with it triggering improved entry points, richer dividend yields, and a double-digit upside potential.

Combined with the growing 5G buildouts along with the robust data center growth, we had believed that the telecom/data center REIT was likely to report robust growth moving forward, resulting in our upgraded Buy rating then.

AMT 1Y Stock Price

Since then, AMT has had an excellent +6.5% stock price performance, well outperforming the wider market at -0.9%, and its telecom/diversified data center REIT peers in varying degrees.

Part of the tailwinds is naturally attributed to its earlier reversal in price trends since September 2024, one similarly observed in its pure-play telecom REIT peer, Crown Castle (CCI), in contrast to its pure-play data center REIT peers which are impacted by the DeepSeek debacle in January 2025.

This is on top of AMT's strategic diversification into the data center end market, which currently comprises 9.6% of its FQ4'24 revenues (+0.2 points QoQ/+0.8 YoY).

Given that CoreSite is already generating double-digit growth for three consecutive years, it is apparent that the 2021 acquisition has triggered a highly strategic expansion of its offerings from telecom to edge compute.

This naturally builds upon AMT's core capabilities as more carriers seek to intensify their 4G networks while rolling out 5G networks, as market experts project a growth in the adoption of 5G networks from 35% of mobile data traffic in 2025 to approximately 80% by 2030.

These reasons are also why the REIT is likely to benefit from the intense wireless capex spending trends moving forward, as similarly guided by numerous telecoms including AT&T (T) and Verizon (VZ).

Combined with the generative AI/data center boom, as market leaders continue to point to the overwhelming demand trends well exceeding available capacity, it is unsurprising that AMT has reported robust FQ4'24 earnings performance.

This is with total property revenue growth to $2.48B (+0.4% QoQ/+2% YoY), organic tenant billings growth by +5% YoY, richer adj EBITDA margins of 66.5% (-0.1 points QoQ/+1 YoY/+4 from FY2019 levels of 62.5%), and AFFO per share of $2.32 (+10.4% YoY).

This is on top of AMT's highly promising FY2025 guidance, with property revenues of $9.99B (+0.6% YoY), organic tenant billings growth by +5% YoY, adj EBITDA margins of 68.8% (+2.3 points YoY), and AFFO per share of $10.40 (+4.4% YoY).

These numbers build upon the robust FY2024 growth at +3% YoY FX neutral, +5.3% YoY, -0.3 points YoY, and +5.8% YoY, respectively, as the management aims to deliver their "long-term guide from '23 out to '27 targeting 5% on average growth rate."

The Consensus Forward Estimates

These reasons are likely why the consensus has raised their forward estimates, with AMT expected to generate an accelerated bottom-line (AFFO per share) growth at a CAGR of +3.5% through FY2027, compared to the prior estimates of +2.4%.

AMT Valuations

For now, AMT's much-needed stock price correction from the September 2024 peaks and the renewed near-term growth opportunities have already triggered its more attractive FWD Price/AFFO valuations of 18.49x, cheaper than the recent peak of 22.94x and its 10Y Price/AFFO mean of 21.80x.

The same conclusion may also be derived after comparing to its data center REIT peers, including Equinix, Inc. (EQIX) at FWD Price/AFFO valuations of 24.33x, Iron Mountain Incorporated (IRM) at 18.62x, and Digital Realty Trust, Inc. (DLR) at 24.64x, aside from its telecom REIT peer, CCI at 13.24x.

This development naturally offers interested investors with an improved margin of safety, especially given AMT's healthier balance sheet with a lower net-debt-to-adj-EBITDA ratio of 5.1x in the latest quarter, compared to 5.2x in FQ3'24 and 5.2x in FQ4'23.

This is on top of the availability of higher liquidity at $12B (+25% YoY), aided by the rather prudent FY2025 capex guidance of $1.68B at the midpoint (-6.6% YoY) and stable shares outstanding on a YoY basis, underscoring the REIT's ability to deliver sustainable growth during an elevated interest rate environment without diluting long-term shareholders.

These developments underscore why the new management team appointed in early 2024 has delivered extremely satisfactory results against the aggressive "priorities centered on balance sheet stream, efficiency, portfolio quality, and capital allocation discipline, all meant to further enhance our customer value proposition and strengthen the durability and quality of earnings for our shareholders over the long term."

So, Is AMT Stock A Buy, Sell, Or Hold?

AMT 5Y Stock Price

These reasons are likely why AMT has had a promising stock price rally to retest the 100/200-day moving averages, as the market welcomes the robust FY2025 guidance.

Even so, it is apparent that the stock is still trading below our fair value estimate of $216.90 offered in our last article, based on the management's FY2024 AFFO per share guidance of $9.95 (actual $9.96) and the prior FWD Price/AFFO valuations of 21.24x (not too far from the 10Y Price/AFFO mean of 21.80x).

Based on the consensus FY2027 AFFO per share estimates of $11.66, there remains a decent upside potential of +14.1% to our long-term price target of $247.60 as well.

This is on top of AMT's guidance of resuming dividend growth "in the mid-single-digit range subject to Board approval, which corresponds to an approximately $3.2 billion distribution to our shareholders."

This implies a potential hike of +4.9% to an annualized payout of $6.83 in 2025, after a year of flat payouts in 2024, with it well exceeding our prior estimated FY2025 dividend payout of $6.80 per share discussed in our last article.

As a result of the robust dual-pronged prospective returns through capital appreciation and dividend incomes, we are reiterating our Buy rating for AMT.

Interested investors may want to observe the price movement for a little longer before adding, since it remains to be seen if the stock may sustain its one-day gains attributed to the uncertain macroeconomic outlook as the Fed also offers a hawkish commentary.

With the borrowing cost normalization likely to be prolonged, we believe that AMT is likely to pull back to its established uptrend support line observed since early January 2025, with it implying a -6% downside from current levels.

Patience may be more prudent in the meantime.

data.title: Precigen: UltraCAR-T Development Brings Another Side Of Pipeline Advancement
data.description:
The last time I wrote about Precigen (NASDAQ:PGEN) it was with respect to a Seeking Alpha article entitled "Precigen: RRP Targeting With AdenoVerse Technology Platform". In this article, I wrote about the opportunity for this company to file a Biologics License Application [BLA] filing of PRGN-2012 for the treatment of patients with recurrent respiratory papillomatosis [RRP]. I'm happy to state that not only was such a regulatory filing submitted to the FDA, but was accepted by it with a Priority Review PDUFA date of August 27th of 2025. The future of this program bodes well, because for the time being the FDA has not set an advisory panel to review this drug. While this is a good program for investors to focus on in the near-term, I believe that great potential lies with another type of technology in the company's pipeline.

This would be with respect to the development of UltraCAR-T to target and kill cancers in a far more effective manner. The gist of this is that the technology, if deployed with clinical success, would disrupt the CAR-T landscape. I will be going over several reasons why below, but the promise of this is another aspect that investors should keep an eye on going forward. This program can be divided into two different advancements. There is UltraCAR-T PRGN-3006 which already had enrollment completed for a phase 1b study targeting patients with acute myeloid leukemia [AML]. Matter of fact, the company is set to speak to the FDA on next steps for this program advancement.

The other UltraCAR-T in the pipeline is PRGN-3008 which is set to target CD19 tumors in oncology and has versatility to also be used to target patients with autoimmune disorders. Preclinical data presented established proof-of-concept of this candidate achieving substantially superior efficacy compared to conventional CD19 CAR-T. The only thing to consider though, for the time being, is that the company is only set to advance its Ultra-CART platform with a partner. The reason why is because in August of 2024 it established a pipeline reprioritization where it would only put its sole focus on the commercialization efforts of PRGN-2012 for the treatment of patients with RRP. However, at the same time, it did highlight that it is in discussions with potential partners to get these UltraCAR-T candidates developed.

PRGN Submission Being Accepted With No Advisory Panel Bodes Well For Potential Approval

As I stated above, Precigen was able to submit its Biologics License Application [BLA] of PRGN-2012 for the treatment of patients with recurrent respiratory papillomatosis [RRP]. This was done on the basis with Accelerated Approval only with a pivotal phase 1/2 study that was completed, which used this AdenoVerse gene therapy to treat these patients. Before going over the data from this pivotal study, plus any catalysts to be released for this specific program, it is first important to go over what this disorder is and what the possible market opportunity for the company could be. Recurrent Respiratory Papillomatosis [RRP] is a type of disorder characterized as the growth of benign tumors that grow in a person's respiratory tract. Even more pronounced, they can sometimes also grow in the voice box [larynx] of a patient as well. What is responsible for causing this disease would be that of human papillomavirus [HPV]. More specifically, HPV6 and HPV11, which are responsible for the formation of such benign tumors. There are two ways whereby this disorder can come about, which are either through sexual contact or passed along from mother to child during birth. There are a host of symptoms that patients with RRP might experience and they are as follows:

  • Not being able to breathe effectively
  • Chronic cough being present
  • Change in voice
  • Growth of benign tumors obstructing air passage way
  • Potential Growth of invasive cancers

In terms of market opportunity for this indication, it can be very large. This would be based on what the company notes in its presentation on slide 9, where it indicates how many patients it could go after in the United States. Plus, the number of RRP patients it could treat with PRGN-2012 in other parts of the world. Such numbers are as follows:

  • 27,000 adult RRP patients in the United States
  • Greater than 125,000 patients ex-U.S.

The current standard of care [SOC] for treating these RRP patients, believe it or not, is surgery. The attempt to remove these benign growths is what is currently available for them. If this is the case, then why would they instead need a treatment like PRGN-2012? It is because surgery doesn't help at all, because there is a high rate of recurrence afterwards. Not only that, but some patients might even need to have surgery every few weeks. The lucky ones might be able to go for over a year or more, but they will eventually need surgery again. With no FDA approved drug in place for these RRP patients, this company has a chance to be the first to give these patients one with PRGN-2012.

The reason why the company was able to submit a rolling Biologics License Application [BLA] submission of PRGN-2012 to treat these RRP patients was on the basis of a phase 1/2 pivotal study, as I noted directly above. This particular trial recruited up to a total of 35 patients. Patients that were recruited into this trial received 4 injections of 5X10^11 particle units [PU] before receiving debulking surgery. That is, this drug was given as an adjuvant before the patients had some parts of their benign tumor removed through surgery. In essence, "debulking" is removing as much as the tumor as possible in place without completely eradicating it. The primary endpoint being looked at for this study was the number of patients who achieved a complete response after the debulking surgery was done. For a patient to be considered to have achieved a complete response, they would need to have no surgical intervention for at least 12 months following treatment. The data was highly ideal, because in the end it was noted that approximately 18 out of 35 patients [51%] of them achieved such a Complete Response [CR]. Not only that, but a huge majority of them [86%] of them had reduced surgical interventions in the year after treatment with PRGN-2012. This, compared to the prior year where such patients had to have a lot more surgical interventions to remove benign tumor growths. This efficacy was achieved with no treatment related adverse events [TRAEs] being above Grade 2, which is another bonus of this drug.

The acceptance of the BLA filing was highly ideal, not just because it was accepted by the FDA, but it was done so with Priority Review. Having said that, the U.S. agency set a PDUFA date of August 27, 2025. What's even better, which I believe increases the odds of approval, would be that no advisory panel has been set to go over review of the drug for the treatment of these patients with RRP. While this doesn't guarantee U.S. marketing approval of PRGN-2012, it does at least remove a hurdle that would have otherwise been in the way. I must reiterate that these patients need a new treatment option, because they have to go through surgery on a constant basis. Not only does it increase the risk of damage to the larynx, but could also result in other morbidities.

UltraCAR-T Program In Place Provides Another Shot On Goal

It remains to be seen whether or not the company receives FDA approval of PRGN-2012 for the treatment of patients with RRP, but it does have another program in its pipeline that is quite promising to the say the least. This would be in terms of it developing UltraCAR-Ts. The promising aspect of this technology, should it be successful, would be for it to completely change the CAR-T landscape. Consider that there is an ability to enhance each problem that remains with CAR-Ts currently available or still in clinical development. Such advantages using its UltraCAR-T platform technology could be:

  • Non-viral multigenic delivery system - ability to establish large multi-gene cargo load with high efficiency; This allows for 1 day manufacturing compared to autologous and allogenic CAR-Ts that require 4-weeks to manufacture
  • In-vivo expansion and persistence - a problem with current CAR-Ts is that they have no persistence whatsoever. To that end, this company has been able to incorporate a membrane bound IL-15 that is responsible for promoting T-cell expansion and persistence

The thing about the UltraCAR-T in place is that it has a "kill switch" deployed. This is put in place to eliminate the CAR-Ts if necessary to improve upon safety of it. The end result of this technology is to eliminate T-cell exhaustion and hopefully increase response rates. All of this, which as I described above, to provide an autologous CAR-T type treatment that is capable of being manufactured in one day. The company had noted that enrollment for a phase 1b study was completed with PRGN-3006 targeting patients with AML. The company hopes to engage with the FDA on potential next steps for this program. Another UltraCAR-T is known as PRGN-3008 and it is being developed to target CD19 directed solid tumors and/or autoimmune disorders. As far as a catalyst goes for this program, it would be whether or not a collaboration or partnership agreement can be made. The company stated in its most recent Q3 of 2024 financial earnings report that it is solely focused on PRGN-2012 advancement and that it is in talks for further financing either through a strategic partnership or another such similar transaction.

Financials

According to the 10-Q SEC Filing, Precigen had cash and cash equivalents of $24.7 million and short-term investments of $3.9 million as of September 30th of 2024. The company was able to close a public offering in August of 2024, which ended up resulting in net proceeds of approximately $30.9 million. The thing is that during the same month the company enacted this offering, it also announced a strategic prioritization of its pipeline. It reduced 20% of its workforce and it chose to only focus on the advancement of PRGN-2012 for the treatment of patients with RRP alone. However, in this 10-Q SEC Filing "on page 27" it notes that it is focused on forming a strategic partnership for its UltraCAR-T program. It is currently in discussions, but there is no guarantee of it being able to complete such a partnership. It also enacted a securities purchase agreement for the sale of 8.00% of its Series A Convertible Perpetual Preferred Stock in a private placement. This helped the company to raise approximately $79 million before deducting expenses. This isn't going to help the company fund itself for a long time either. Why is that? That's because this company burns roughly $36.4 million of cash per quarter.

Risks To Business

There are several risks that investors should be aware of before investing in Precigen. The first risk to consider would be in terms of the BLA filing of PRGN-2012 for the treatment of patients with recurrent respiratory papillomatosis. The risk here is that despite a Priority Review date being set by the FDA, there is no assurance that the company is going to receive U.S. marketing approval of this drug for the treatment of these patients.

The second risk to consider would be in terms of the advancement of PRGN-3006 for the treatment of patients with Acute Myeloid Leukemia [AML]. With the company having completed enrollment in the phase 1b study using this UltraCAR-T to target these patients, it is set to meet with the FDA to discuss next potential steps. The risk here is that there is no assurance that the FDA is going to like the data it has seen thus far. Nor, that it will allow Precigen to move forward in using this clinical candidate to treat these AML patients in a subsequent study.

The third and final risk to consider would be in terms of the development of UltraCAR-T PRGN-3008 as a next generation CAR-T. The goal of the company is to be able to develop this candidate to target both CD19 expressing solid tumors and autoimmune disorders. Thus, the first risk here is that there is no guarantee that this type of CAR-T is going to work in either of these two general indication categories. Another risk is that there is no assurance that the company will be able to advance either of these programs in the clinic. As I stated beforehand, the company won't advance its UltraCAR-Ts unless it can find a partner who is willing to fund it. Lastly, the whole point of the UltraCAR-T technology is to have several of the advantages that I listed above. Being able to generate persistence, safety/kill switch, IL-15 membrane binding, increased manufacturing time frame and others. Thus, the risk here is that despite all of these enhancements, there is no assurance the company will ultimately yield superior CAR-T candidates over other biotechs in the same space. Nor, that such modifications are going to allow the company to report upon an improved clinical outcome either.

Conclusion

Precigen has done well to advance the use of PRGN-2012 for the treatment of patients with recurrent respiratory papillomatosis or RRP. That's because not only did it get the FDA to accept its rolling BLA submission of this gene therapy candidate, but it was able to do so with Priority Review with a PDUFA date of August 27, 2025. The hope is that it is going to be able to find a partner that will be willing to fund its UltraCAR-T program though. The reason why is because of what I listed above, in that this technology is very promising with all the modifications that it has been able to do with CAR-T development. I think that investors should be particularly intrigued with the fact that it might be able to deploy autologous CAR-Ts such as PRGN-3008, which can be manufactured in 1 day. Again, other CAR-Ts have a manufacturing time frame of about 4-weeks. With the BLA submission of PRGN-2012 being accepted by the FDA with a Priority Review date of August 27th of 2025, plus the unique nature of its UltraCAR-T program it has in place, I believe that investors could benefit from any potential gains made here.
